titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/174

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=29 status=draft modified=2023-06-29 15:38:40.255091+00 | translation moa=30 status=draft modified=2023-06-29 15:38:41.470387+00

div.p:
Alexander . dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitis . Baronibus . Iustic’ . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . Clericis et laicis 
Salutem . Sciant presentes et Futuri Me concessisse . et hac carta mea confirmasse Ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . Donationem quam Walterus .
filius Alani . fecit eidem Ecclesie et Monachis . de terra de Mauhelin’ . et de pastura in Foresto eius . Et de una Carrucata terre uersus terram de Duueglas . Et de
piscatura in hostio fluminis . AR . Et de terra de Ædmundestun’ . Et de viginti acris terre cum Tofto de Berewic’ . Item . totam terram illam de Eskedale . per diuisas
que nominantur et continentur in Cartis Roberti . Auenel’ . et Geruasii heredis ipsius . Item . totam terram illam quam Iohnannes filius Orm’ . eis dedit in Territorio de Hunedun’ .
per ipsas Rectas diuisas que continentur in Carta predicti Iohannis quam habent de eo de eadem terra . Item . totam terram infra Feodum de Hunum a Riuulo de Quindenhope
sursum per semitam illam usque ad fossatum inter Raschawe et Cudhbrichteshope . et abinde per easdem Rectas diuisas que continentur in Carta Willelmi filii Iohannis . quam
habent de eo . Item . Donationem quam Osulfus filius vctredi . et vctredus filius eius contulerunt eis de terra de Rigwude . Item . Donationem quam Cospatricius
Comes fecit eisdem Monachis de terra de Hertesheued . et spot . et de quadam salina . Item . Donationem quam Willelmus de Bruis . eis fecit de quadam piscaria iuxta
Ecclesiam de Reinpatric’ . cum una acra terre et pastura quatuor vaccarum . et sex Boum . et aliis aisiamentis uille de Reinpatric’ . Item . Duas Bouatas terre . et Duas
acras Prati . cum pastura ad quadringentas oues que Abbas et Conuentus de Calchou . eis dederunt nomine pacis inter eos facte . Item . totam terram illam quam habent
de Patricio . de Ridale in territorio de Whittun’ . per illas diuisas que continentur in carta Patricii . de Ridale . quam habent de eo de eadem terra . Item . Donationem quam Ricardus .
de Moreuill’ . et Auicia uxor eius fecerunt ipsis Monachis de Capella de Parco . cum terra ei adiacente . Et preterea de tota terra de Milkenesside . per illas Rectas diuisas que in
Carta predicti Ricardi . et Auicie uxoris eius continentur . Item . totam terram et pasturam quam Willelmus de Moruill’ . eis dedit in augmentum predicte terre de Milkenesside per Rectas di
uisas nominatas in Carta eiusdem Willelmi . Item . totam illam partem terre in territorio de kilbewocistun’ . quam Alanus filius Rollandi . eis dedit per Rectas diuisas nominatas in Carta ipsius Alani .
in excambium terre quam Willelmus de Moreuill’ . eis diuisit in Cunigham’ . cum communi pastura et aliis aisiamentis predicte uille . sicut in carta ipsius Alani . continetur . Item . Terram
quam Walterus . Corbet’ . filius Walteri . Corbet’ . eis dedit in territorio de Cliftun’ . per Rectas diuisas nominatas in carta eiusdem Walteri . Corbet’ . quam habent de eo de eadem terra . Item . illam
dimidiam Carrucatam terre . in terra et prato . quam Anselmus de Wichetun’ . eis dedit de feodo suo de Molle . per easdem Rectas diuisas que continentur in carta ipsius anselmi . quam ipsi Mona
chi habent de eo . Item . Donationem illam quam Comes Patricius . eis fecit de tota terra arabili que uocatur Sorwelesfeld’ . ultra Ledre ex occidentali parte uersus Grangiam predictorum Monachorum
sicut Willelmus Sorweles eam plenius tenuit . Item . dimidiam Carrucatam terre quam Ricardus . de Londoniis . eis dedit in lessedwin’ . sicut Carta ipsius Ricardi . eis testatur . Concessi etiam eis
quietam clamantiam quam Dauid . Olifard’ . et Walterus . filius . et heres eius fecerunt deo . et Ecclesie de Melros et predictis Monachis de Hertesheued . et de spot . sicut in Carta domini Regis . Willelmi
patris mei continetur . Concessi etiam amicabilem compositionem factam inter Comitem . Patricium . de Dunbar’ . et predictos Monachos super controuersia que [ue]rtebatur inter eos de pastura uersus occiden
tem de ledre . sicut scripta inde facta testantur . Concessi etiam eis . [q]uietam clamantiam quam Alanus filius Walteri . eis fecit de calumpnia que fuit inter eos . de pastura ex occidentali parte de ledre . Quare volo et precipio . vt predicti Monachi omnes has predictas possessiones habeant et teneant in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam . Ita libere et quiete .
plenarie et honorifice  sicut Carte donatorum et confirmationes predecessorum meorum testantur . saluo seruicio meo . Testibus . Willelmo de Boscho Cancellario . meo . Seihero .
de Quinci . Comite . Wintonie . Comite . Malcolmo . de Fif’ . Philippo . de Valoniis . Camerario . meo . Willelmo de Valoniis . filio . eius . Roberto . Capellano meo . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Dauid . Ma
rescallo . Thoma . de Lundin’ . hostiario . meo . Heruico . Marescallo . Iordano . Cumin’ . Apud Edenburg’ . iii . die Aprilis . * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *

p.cl: Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to the bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting:

Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter confirmed, to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the donation that Walter son of Alan made to the same church and monks, of the land of Mauchline, and of pasture in his forest; and of one ploughgate of land towards the land of Douglas, and of a fishery at the mouth of the River Ayr, and of the land of Edmonston, and of twenty acres of land with a toft at Berwick. Also, the whole of the land that John son of Orm gave to them in the territory of Hownam, by those right bounds that are contained in the charter of the aforesaid John that they have of him from the same land. Also, the whole land within the feu of Hownam, from the Capehope Burn upwards by that pathway to the boundary between Raeshaw and Cuthberthope, and from there by the same right bounds that are described in the charter of William the son of John, which they have from him. Also, the donation of the land of Ringwood that Osulf the son of Uhtred, and Uhtred his son, bestowed to them. Also, the donation that Cosptric made the same monks of the land of Hartside, and Spott, and a saltpan. Also, the donation that William de Bruce made to them of a fishery near the church of Rainpatrick, with one acre of land and pasture for four cows, and six oxen, and other easements of the toun of Rainpatrick. Also, two oxgangs of land, and two acres of meadow, with pasture for four hundred ewes that the abbot and convent of Kelso gave to them as a token of the peace made between them. Also, the whole land that they have from Patrick of Ryedale in the territory of Whitton, by those bounds that are contained in the charter of Patrick of Ryedale, which they have from him concerning the same land. Also, the donation that Richard de Morville and Avicia his wife made of the chapel of Park to the same monks, with the land belonging to it; and in addition, of the land of Milkenesside, by its right bounds, which are contained in the charter of the aforesaid Richard and Avicia his wife. Also, the whole land and pasture that William de Morville gave to them to enlarge the aforesaid land of Milkenesside by the right bounds described in Williams’s charter. Also, the whole portion of land in the territory of Kilbewocistun, which Alan son of Roland gave them, by the right bounds described in Alan’s charter, in exchange for the land that William de Morville apportioned to them in Cunningham, with common pasture and other easements of the aforesaid toun, just as it is contained in Alan’s charter. Also, the land that Walter Corbet, son of Walter Corbet, gave them in the territory of Clifton, by the right bounds described in the charter of the same Walter Corbet that they have from him concerning the same land. Also, that half-ploughgate of land, in land and meadow, which Anselm of Whitton gave them from his feu of Mow, by the same rights bounds that are contained in Anslem’s charter that the monks have from him. Also, the donation that Earl Patrick made to them of the whole arable land called Sorrowlessfield beyond Leader from the western side towards the grange of the aforesaid monks, just as William Sorrowless fully held it. Also, a half-ploughgate of the land that Richard of London gave them in Lessudden, just as Richard’s charter to them bears witness. I have also granted to them the quit claim that David Oliphant and Walter his son and heir made to God and the church of Melrose and the aforesaid monks, of Hartside, and of Spott, just as it is contained in the charter of the lord king, William my father. I have also granted the friendly agreement made between Earl Patrick of Dunbar and the aforesaid monks over the controversy that arose between them concerning the pasture to the west of the Leader, just as the writing made on the matter bears witness. I have also granted them the quit claim that Alan son of Walter made to them for the claim there was between them on the pasture on the west side of the Leader. For this reason I will and command that the aforesaid monks shall have and hold all these aforesaid possessions in free and perpetual alms, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charters of the donors and the confirmations of my predecessors bear witness, saving my service.

As witnesses: William del Bois my chancellor, Saer de Quincy earl of Winchester, Earl Malcolm of Fife, Philip de Valgones my chamberlain, William de Valognes his son, Robert my chaplain, John of Maxwell, David the marischal, Thomas of Lundie my doorward, Hervey the marischal, Jordan Comyn.
At Edinburgh on the third day of April.
